comment_text.sn14.2:1.2: The use of the word paṭicca here connects back to dependent origination. Narrowing in on the particulars of sense experience, the text shows that particular factors give rise to other correlated factors, i.e. “this gives rise to this” (SN 12.37:2.2).

root_text.sn14.2:1.4: Cakkhudhātu sotadhātu ghānadhātu jivhādhātu kāyadhātu manodhātu—
root_text.sn14.2:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ?
root_text.sn14.2:2.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattan”ti.

translation_text.sn14.2:1.2: “Mendicants, diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts.
translation_text.sn14.2:1.3: And what is the diversity of elements?
translation_text.sn14.2:1.4: The eye element, ear element, nose element, tongue element, body element, and mind element.
translation_text.sn14.2:1.5: This is called the diversity of elements.
translation_text.sn14.2:2.1: And how does diversity of elements give rise to diversity of contacts?
translation_text.sn14.2:2.2: The eye element gives rise to eye contact.
translation_text.sn14.2:2.7: The mind element gives rise to mind contact.
translation_text.sn14.2:2.8: That’s how diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts.”
